sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: RHP Maintained by Wells Fargo -- Price Target Raised to $136
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has maintained an Overweight rating for Ryman Hospitality Properties (RHP) and increased its price target from $126.00 to $136.00. Despite this positive analyst sentiment, GuruFocus indicates RHP is currently overvalued, with a GF Value™ of $108.12 compared to its current price of $130.34. The company's GF Score™ of 81/100 points to strong overall performance, though its financial strength is relatively weak.

feed.title: 44,963 Shares in Arrow Electronics, Inc. $ARW Bought by Towle & Co.
feed.summary: Towle & Co. initiated a new position in Arrow Electronics (NYSE:ARW) during the first quarter, acquiring 44,963 shares valued at approximately $6.45 million, making it their 18th-largest holding. This comes as Arrow Electronics reported strong quarterly results, beating analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and authorized a $1 billion share repurchase program. Despite some insider selling, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $222.00 for the company.

feed.title: Is AXT (AXTI) Using Its New Board Seat to Reframe Its China Governance Story?
feed.summary: AXT, Inc. recently appointed Jia-Bin Duh as an independent Class III director to its board, aiming to leverage his extensive experience in Greater China's technology sector. This move is expected to reshape AXT's investment narrative, particularly concerning its China-focused operations and governance, as the company navigates profitability challenges, export permits, and market concentration risks. While Duh's appointment adds valuable expertise, upcoming Q2 2026 earnings will be crucial to assess operational improvements and the board's enhanced oversight.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Stock Climbs 23% On A 5-Day Winning Streak
feed.summary: Seagate Technology (STX) stock has surged 22.5% over five consecutive trading days, adding an estimated $37 billion to its market value, bringing its total to about $202 billion. Despite its impressive revenue growth of 28.9% and operating margin of 29.5%, which outpace the S&P 500, its high price-to-earnings multiple of 84.9 suggests a demanding valuation. The article emphasizes that while such streaks grab market attention, investors should analyze the underlying business fundamentals against the current price.

feed.title: BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) Sets New 1-Year Low - Here's Why
feed.summary: BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) shares reached a new 52-week low of $2.76, closing near $2.76 with over 3.1 million shares traded on Friday. The company has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $5.50, despite some recent downgrades and reaffirmed "Sell" ratings. Insider selling by Director Dorothy D. Hayes and CFO Sean Raymond Ricker has also been noted, while institutional ownership remains relatively low at 7.55%.

feed.title: SLB soars 10% on strong activity outside Middle East war disruptions (SLB:NYSE)
feed.summary: SLB shares surged over 10% after reporting better-than-expected Q2 adjusted earnings and projecting a 3-4% sequential revenue increase for Q3. Despite a 14% drop in Middle East and Asia Q2 revenue due to regional conflicts, the company sees improving activity and anticipates its data center solutions business to exceed $1 billion in annualized revenue by year-end. This strong performance, especially outside the Middle East, instilled investor confidence.

feed.title: Western Digital Corp Stock (WDC) Moved Down by 5.14% on Jul 24: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Western Digital Corp (WDC) shares declined by 5.14% on July 24, primarily due to weak quarterly guidance from semiconductor and storage industry peers, indicating a broader cooling in the sector. Investor sentiment is further dampened by uncertainties surrounding the company's planned structural reorganization of its HDD and Flash businesses, as well as macroeconomic pressures like higher interest rates. The stock faces a "risk-off" environment until management provides clearer operational guidance and updates on the corporate split.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC Stock (STX) Moved Down by 3.78% on Jul 24: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC (STX) stock declined by 3.78% on July 24th due to disappointing quarterly results, cautious market recovery statements, and reduced enterprise demand for storage. The company faces implementation hurdles for next-generation recording technologies and concerns over its debt-to-equity ratio, leading institutional investors to lower their exposure. Macroeconomic factors like interest rate paths and geopolitical tensions further contribute to the stock's volatility and a more conservative growth outlook.

feed.title: Nvidia, Microsoft lead call for open-weight AI models after Kimi
feed.summary: A coalition of tech companies including Nvidia and Microsoft is advocating for open-weight AI models to ensure the US maintains its technological leadership. They argue that this approach, which allows for customization and accessibility, is crucial for continued innovation and widespread adoption of AI. This push comes shortly after a Chinese startup released a competitive open-weight AI model, highlighting the intensifying global race in AI development.

feed.title: Themes Silver Miners ETF (AGMI) stock price, news, quote and history
feed.summary: This article provides comprehensive financial information for the Themes Silver Miners ETF (AGMI), including its current stock price, historical data, performance metrics, and key holdings. It details the ETF's objective to track global silver mining companies and summarizes its expense ratio and net assets. The content also features related tickers and other ETFs for comparative analysis.

feed.title: ACCO Brands Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.075 per Share, Payable Sept. 9 to Holders of Record Aug. 21
feed.summary: ACCO Brands announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.075 per share. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on September 9 to shareholders who are on record as of August 21. The announcement was made on July 24, 2026, at 09:37 am EDT.

feed.title: Hubbell declares $1.42 quarterly dividend per share By Investing.com
feed.summary: Hubbell Incorporated (NYSE:HUBB) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share on its common stock. This dividend will be paid on September 15, 2026, to shareholders recorded as of August 31, 2026. Hubbell specializes in utility and electrical solutions and reported revenues of $5.8 billion in 2025.

feed.title: Why Lucid Group Stock Is Tanking Today
feed.summary: Lucid Group (NASDAQ: LCID) shares dropped by about 7% due to increasing legal scrutiny and a new "Strong Sell" rating from an analyst. The company is facing a proposed securities fraud class action related to its weaker first quarter performance, and analysts are concerned about production issues, cash burn, and a challenging financial outlook, despite ongoing financial backing from its largest shareholder.

feed.title: Progressive (PGR) Stock Rises 3% Following Upgrade from Morgan S
feed.summary: Progressive (PGR) stock rose 3% after Morgan Stanley upgraded the property and casualty insurer to Equalweight, citing stable trends and a valuation reset. Despite recent insider selling, Progressive's strong GF Score of 90/100, robust growth potential, and a P/E ratio near its five-year low suggest it may be an attractive investment in the insurance sector. The company has outperformed competitors in premium growth and holds a significant market position.

feed.title: EBAY Showered Owners With Cash. The Stock Did Not Cooperate
feed.summary: Over the past five years, eBay has returned approximately $20 billion in cash to shareholders through buybacks and dividends, an amount equal to 41% of its current market value. Despite these substantial payouts, the stock's five-year total return of 75% has lagged the S&P 500's 81% gain, raising questions about the investment's performance for holders. The article explores whether eBay's cash-return strategy signals a mature business lacking high-growth opportunities or a disciplined management, amidst increasing competition in its core C2C market.

feed.title: ExxonMobil Stock Rides A 6-Day Winning Streak To A 8.6% Gain
feed.summary: ExxonMobil's stock has achieved a 6-day winning streak, resulting in an 8.6% gain and adding $52 billion to its market value. Despite this recent surge, the article notes that the company's fundamental performance metrics, such as revenue growth and operating margin, trail behind the S&P 500 medians. Investors are advised to examine the business's fundamentals rather than solely relying on short-term momentum.

feed.title: Insulet examines mental health toll of diabetes in partnership with meditation app Calm
feed.summary: Insulet, a medical device company, has partnered with the meditation app Calm to address the mental health challenges faced by people with diabetes. This collaboration aims to provide mindfulness content specifically for diabetes patients, as research indicates a significant need for mental health support among this group due to stress, anxiety, and burnout associated with managing the disease. The initiative is part of Insulet's broader strategy to integrate consumer empathy and holistic care into its marketing and product development, as demonstrated by previous campaigns featuring diabetes in pop culture.

feed.title: AMD: Firm Raises the Server CPU Bar Yet Again at Its Advancing AI Event
feed.summary: AMD's recent Advancing AI event showcased new AI products, notably Helios AI racks and Venice CPUs, and significant upward revisions to its AI market forecasts. The company now expects the server CPU total addressable market (TAM) to reach $220 billion by 2030, a substantial increase from previous estimates, and the broader AI accelerator market TAM to hit $1.4 trillion. Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for AMD to $530 per share, citing accelerated server CPU growth assumptions.

feed.title: FedEx, Kroger, UPS, Home Depot class actions allege unpaid wages
feed.summary: Four separate class action lawsuits have been filed against FedEx, Kroger, UPS, and Home Depot, alleging these companies failed to pay employees for mandatory security screening time before and after shifts. The lawsuits, filed in Colorado and Connecticut federal courts, seek unpaid minimum and overtime wages, penalties, interest, and attorneys' fees, claiming these screenings constitute compensable "time worked" under state laws. Additionally, the Kroger and UPS complaints include claims for denied rest breaks and, in Kroger's case, alleged uncompensated meal breaks and equipment gathering time.

feed.title: Hubbell Sets Sept. 15 Date to Pay $1.42 Dividend to Shareholders of Record
feed.summary: Hubbell (NYSE:HUBB) has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share on its common stock. This dividend is payable on September 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of August 31, 2026. The company, a leading manufacturer of utility and electrical solutions, reported $5.8 billion in revenues for 2025.

feed.title: Hubbell Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Hubbell Incorporated (NYSE: HUBB) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share on its common stock. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on September 15, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of August 31, 2026. Hubbell specializes in manufacturing utility and electrical solutions, reporting $5.8 billion in revenues for 2025.

feed.title: $10MM DOWN THE DRAIN- Court Grants Final Approval to Gen Digital’s $9,950,000 TCPA Settlement Disaster
feed.summary: A court has granted final approval to a $9.95 million TCPA settlement against Gen Digital, resulting from a class action lawsuit over wrong-number prerecorded spam calls related to LifeLock and Norton products. The lawsuit, led by prominent TCPA attorneys Mike Greenwald and Anthony Paronich, saw Gen Digital agree to pay out a substantial sum, with over $3.3 million allocated to plaintiff's lawyers. The class definition included individuals in the U.S. who received prerecorded calls about LifeLock or Norton accounts to cellular numbers not assigned to a Gen Digital account holder between February 2021 and October 2025.

feed.title: This Dover Analyst Turns Bulish; Here Are Top 5 Upgrades For Friday
feed.summary: This article highlights five significant stock upgrades issued by top Wall Street analysts on a Friday. Companies receiving upgrades include Dover Corp (DOV), Progressive Corp (PGR), PayPal Holdings Inc (PYPL), Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (DLR), and Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. (CLF), with analysts adjusting their ratings and price targets to reflect a more positive outlook.

feed.title: Cummins announces phased launch plan for 2027
feed.summary: Cummins has announced a phased launch plan for its new X10 and X15 engines for Model Year 2027, based on proposed changes to U.S. EPA emissions regulations. This approach maintains regulatory compliance and supports customer production schedules by allowing a measured transition to its new HELM engine platforms, with full X15 production expected in Q4 2027 and X10 in Q3 2027. The company’s next-generation B platform is still set to launch in January 2028, and current X15, X12, and L9 engines will remain available during the transition.

feed.title: Jefferies Hires Muni Veterans from Goldman and Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group Inc. is expanding its municipal bond team by hiring experienced professionals from Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley. This strategic move indicates Jefferies' focus on strengthening its muni sales and trading capabilities.

feed.title: chipotle
feed.summary: A new Chipotle restaurant has recently opened on Stafford Street in Worcester. The article, published by The Worcester Guardian, briefly announces the opening and is accompanied by a photo of the new establishment. This highlights ongoing business development in the Worcester area.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Could Be 3% Undervalued After Intel Capex Boost
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has gained investor attention following Intel's increased capital expenditure forecast, reinforcing optimism for AI-driven semiconductor tool demand. While a fair value narrative suggests AMAT is 3% undervalued, the Simply Wall St DCF model presents a contrasting view, indicating potential overvaluation if AI enthusiasm wanes. Investors are encouraged to review both perspectives and assess the risks, including potential export restrictions to China or shifts in key customer spending.

feed.title: AXT (NASDAQ:AXTI) Trading Down 7.8% - What's Next?
feed.summary: AXT (NASDAQ:AXTI) shares decreased by 7.8% during Friday's trading, hitting a low of $48.78 with significantly reduced trading volume compared to its average. Despite dropping below the average analyst price target of $43.80, analysts currently hold a "Hold" consensus rating for the stock, though opinions are mixed with some Buy, Hold, and Sell ratings. The company recently exceeded earnings and revenue estimates, but notable insider selling, including shares from the CEO and a director, has occurred.

feed.title: Banks Lending Resurgence Extends to Second Quarter
feed.summary: Major banks, including Bank of America, U.S. Bancorp, PNC Financial Services Group, and Truist Financial, significantly increased their commercial real estate loan balances in Q2 2026, continuing a trend from Q1. This signals a return to CRE lending after a post-pandemic retreat, with banks now focusing on growth sectors like multifamily and data centers while tightening lending standards. Despite the overall resurgence, some banks like Bank OZK are still reducing their exposure to real estate loans.

feed.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Expands Pipeline With New First-in-Human Trial for BMS-986533
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) has initiated a first-in-human Phase 1 trial for BMS-986533, an oral drug, to evaluate its safety, pharmacokinetics, and dosing in healthy volunteers. This randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study includes multiple dosing schemes and assesses interactions with common medications. The trial, which began enrollment on 2026-06-01 and was last updated on 2026-07-23, is an early-stage pipeline expansion for BMY, indicating investment in future assets rather than near-term revenue.

feed.title: BioLife Solutions Investor Alert: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC Investigates Adequacy of Price and Process in Proposed Sale of BioLife Solutions, Inc. - BLFS
feed.summary: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF) is investigating the proposed sale of BioLife Solutions, Inc. (NasdaqCM: BLFS) to Repligen Corporation (NasdaqGS: RGEN). KSF is concerned that the offered consideration of $11.25 in cash and 0.1442 shares of Repligen common stock per BioLife share may undervalue the company. The firm encourages BioLife shareholders who believe the transaction undervalues the company to contact them to discuss their legal rights.

feed.title: BATL Stock Stalls As Tight Range Signals Next Big Move
feed.summary: BATL stock has entered a consolidation phase after a previous spike, trading in a tight range around $1.60-$1.70. While the company reports solid revenue, it struggles with deep net losses and negative profit margins, making it a high-risk, high-reward stock for active traders. The current sideways movement is seen by traders as a potential precursor to a significant breakout or breakdown once a strong catalyst emerges.

feed.title: Hims & Hers Health, Repligen, West Pharmaceutical Services, and 10x Genomics Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Hims & Hers Health, Repligen, West Pharmaceutical Services, and 10x Genomics experienced stock declines following concerns about a rising number of uninsured patients, as highlighted in HCA Healthcare's recent earnings report. HCA reported a "negative payer mix shift" leading to a $400 million reduction in pre-tax income, signaling potential challenges for the broader hospital industry. Hims & Hers Health shares dropped 9.3%, while Repligen, West Pharmaceutical Services, and 10x Genomics also saw declines, raising concerns about broader impact from the healthcare industry trend.

feed.title: Outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite linked to 4 more states
feed.summary: Federal health officials have linked a diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak, initially tied to shredded lettuce from Taco Bell, to four additional states: Illinois, Kansas, Oklahoma, and Pennsylvania. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reports 1,947 cases linked to Taco Bell lettuce, with 98 hospitalizations, and identifies Taylor Farms lettuce as the likely source. This outbreak is part of a record-setting year for cyclospora cases, with over 4,100 confirmed cases and 7,400 likely cases across 41 states since May 1.

feed.title: Digital Realty Trust Soars After Big Earnings Beat And Guidance Hike
feed.summary: Digital Realty Trust Inc. (DLR) experienced a significant stock surge of 13.94% following a substantial beat on its Q2 earnings and an increase in its 2026 guidance. The company's core FFO and revenue outperformed Street expectations, driven by strong data-center demand and AI-driven growth. Analysts responded positively with new Buy ratings and increased price targets, though the company's participation in an AI-power pledge highlights potential future political and cost scrutiny regarding electricity usage.

feed.title: Edwards posts strong TAVR sales ahead of potential Medicare coverage expansion
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences reported stronger-than-expected Q2 sales for its TAVR devices and other heart valve treatments, benefiting from increasing clinical demand. The company expects continued growth driven by a potential Medicare coverage expansion for TAVR in asymptomatic patients and upcoming clinical trial data for moderate aortic stenosis, which could broaden the indication for the treatment. Edwards also raised its full-year sales growth outlook, with new TMTT products and the next-generation Sapien X4S technology poised for future contributions.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems SVP Paul Cunningham Sells 2,000 Shares for $767,000
feed.summary: Paul Cunningham, SVP of Cadence Design Systems, sold 2,000 shares of common stock for approximately $767,000 on July 15, 2026. This transaction was part of a pre-planned Rule 10b5-1 trading plan and represents about 2% of his direct holdings, suggesting continued confidence in the company's future despite the sale. Cadence Design Systems demonstrates strong operational leverage and profitability, with significant growth potential driven by AI and high-performance computing demand.

feed.title: HCA Monitoring Report for 2025, Two Areas of Potential Non-Compliance Noted
feed.summary: Dogwood Health Trust and its Independent Monitor have released their annual compliance monitoring report for HCA Healthcare, Inc. (HCA) regarding Mission Health System, identifying two potential instances of non-compliance for calendar year 2025. These issues relate to the continuation of services (degradation and cessation of certain services) and maintaining good standing in Medicare and Medicaid, following a temporary notice of termination for Mission Hospital. The organizations will host a public Q&A webinar on August 4, 2026, to discuss the findings and process.

feed.title: Amazon Prime settlement: How eligible customers can claim their refund
feed.summary: Eligible Amazon Prime customers have until July 27, 2026, to file claims for refunds of up to $51 as part of a $2.5 billion settlement with the Federal Trade Commission. The settlement addresses allegations that Amazon enrolled consumers without informed consent and made cancellations difficult. Customers who enrolled through specific challenged methods or struggled to cancel between June 23, 2019, and June 23, 2025, and used between three and ten Prime benefits, may qualify for a refund.

feed.title: Ally Financial sees loan applications rise to record levels in Q2
feed.summary: Ally Financial experienced a record 4.6 million credit applications in Q2, leading to $13.3 billion in retail auto originations, a 21% increase year-over-year. By encouraging dealers to send them all applications, Ally can be more selective while still growing volume. The company's Q2 originations included substantial increases in used and new auto loans, although lease originations declined, partly due to changes in EV tax credits.

feed.title: Ford, China’s Geely to build cars in Spain in joint venture
feed.summary: Ford and China's Geely Auto are forming a joint venture to manufacture vehicles at Ford's Valencia, Spain plant, with production slated to begin in 2028. The partnership aims to maximize plant utilization and scale economies, producing new Ford multi-energy vehicles and Geely electric SUVs. An analyst warns that while the deal offers Ford a short-term lifeline amidst cost pressures, it could significantly strengthen Geely's long-term position in the critical European EV market.

feed.title: Apple Inc Stock (AAPL) Moved Up by 3.55% on Jul 24: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock rose by 3.55% on July 24, driven by positive institutional sentiment, optimistic supply chain indicators, and the integration of advanced AI capabilities. Analysts have revised revenue forecasts upward due to strong performance in emerging markets and spatial computing. Despite potential risks like antitrust litigation and geopolitical vulnerabilities, the market is focusing on Apple's ecosystem synergy, free cash flow, and anticipated product cycle upgrades.

feed.title: T-Mobile US Inc Stock (TMUS) Moved Up by 4.96% on Jul 24: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: T-Mobile US Inc (TMUS) stock rose by 4.96% following second-quarter earnings that beat estimates for revenue and EPS. The company also raised its full-year guidance for core adjusted EBITDA and adjusted free cash flow, and increased its share repurchase authorization and quarterly dividend. These factors, alongside strategic ventures into fiber and bullish analyst sentiment, are driving the positive market reaction for TMUS.

feed.title: Wyndham Delivers Strong Q2 With Record Pipeline and Higher RevPAR
feed.summary: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts reported a strong second quarter with a 2 percent increase in U.S. RevPAR and a record global pipeline of 261,000 rooms. The company's net income rose 17 percent to $102 million, reflecting confidence in its brands despite weaker international performance and the impact of the Revo insolvency. Wyndham also launched a native ChatGPT app, becoming the first major U.S. economy and midscale hotel franchiser to do so.

feed.title: Top US Oilfield Services Firm SLB Beats Quarterly Profit Estimates
feed.summary: SLB, a leading U.S. oilfield services firm, surpassed its second-quarter profit expectations despite a 14% drop in revenue from the Middle East and Asia due to disruptions from the Iran war. The company's overall revenue climbed to $8.97 billion, driven by a significant 36% increase in North American activity, including higher offshore operations and a rebound in U.S. shale drilling. SLB posted an adjusted profit of 55 cents per share, exceeding analysts' estimates of 51 cents.

feed.title: Months after Syracuse ditched Flock, critics say switch to Axon solved nothing
feed.summary: Syracuse city leaders switched from Flock Safety to Axon Enterprise for license plate readers due to privacy concerns and data-sharing issues. However, critics, including Daniel Schwarz of the New York Civil Liberties Union, argue that the switch has not resolved privacy risks, as Flock cameras remain installed and the new Axon system presents similar dangers through its extensive surveillance technology and AI reliance. Schwarz also noted that the Surveillance Technology Working Group has been sidelined, and requests for the new policy and contract have gone unanswered, highlighting a lack of transparency and accountability in the new system.

feed.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT (ARR) Looks Cheap As Q2 Profit Turns Around
feed.summary: ARMOUR Residential REIT (ARR) reported a significant turnaround in Q2 2026, with a net income of US$114.82 million compared to losses in the prior year, despite a 10.83% year-to-date share price decline. The stock trades at a P/E ratio of 10x, making it cheaper than the broader US market and its Mortgage REIT peers, suggesting it may be undervalued. While profitability and return on equity are strong, risks include weak long-term total returns and dividend coverage concerns.

feed.title: Video recorded from homeowner's porch shows sound emitted '24/7' by massive Michigan data center
feed.summary: Residents in Dowagiac, Michigan, are suing Alliance Cloud Services, a subsidiary of Hyperscale Data Inc., over constant and intense noise emanating from a 30-megawatt data center. The plaintiffs allege the noise from cooling equipment is a nuisance, invades their property, reduces home values, and negatively impacts their quality of life, preventing them from using their yard. They are seeking class action certification and damages, and the lawsuit comes as Hyperscale Data plans to significantly expand the facility's capacity.

feed.title: Zevra Therapeutics Receives EMA Negative Opinion on Arimoclomol Marketing Application
feed.summary: Zevra Therapeutics announced that the European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) issued a negative opinion on its Marketing Authorization Application for arimoclomol, a treatment for Niemann-Pick disease type C (NPC). The company plans to request a re-examination of this decision. While this is a regulatory setback for potential European commercialization, Zevra continues to support eligible patients through its global Expanded Access Program.

feed.title: Moderna stock may move 4.2% on July 31 earnings release By Investing.com
feed.summary: Moderna Inc. (MRNA) stock is projected to move 4.2% around its July 31 earnings release, according to options data. Historically, the stock has exceeded its implied move in three of the last eight earnings announcements. Notably, its largest post-earnings move in the past two years was a 20.3% fall on August 1, 2024, surpassing the 7.9% move priced by options.
